commonIssues:
Mismatches in names, areas, or land types across old documents, causing rejection during registration.

How to fix:

  • Apply for correction of records through the MP Bhulekh portal or the local Tehsildar. MP Bhulekh

Difficulty tracing ownership for 30 years, crucial for safe, legal acquisition.

How to fix:

  • Conduct a search via the SAMPADA portal to trace the ownership history. SAMPADA MP

Buying agricultural land without a "Diversion Certificate" from the Town and Country Planning Department, making it illegal for residential use.

How to fix:

  • Apply for a Diversion Certificate via the RCMS (Revenue Case Management System) portal. RCMS MP

Failure to obtain NOCs from Gram Panchayats or municipality approvals for building plans.

How to fix:

  • Obtain the necessary NOCs from the local Gram Panchayat or Municipal Corporation.

Incorrect information, such as signature mismatches, typos in plot numbers, or missing identification documents.

How to fix:

  • Ensure all details in the deed match the Khasra and Aadhaar before booking an appointment.

Significant delays in updating land records after a sale, often due to high volume or backlogs in the Revenue Department.

How to fix:

  • Track your mutation application online on MP Bhulekh. If delayed, file an appeal via RCMS.

Issues arise when attempting to mutate property based on a Will, which requires extensive legal proof beyond the capability of local Tehsildars.

How to fix:

  • Ensure the Will is registered, and file a formal mutation application with the Tehsildar along with a death certificate.

Difficulties in navigating the MP Bhulekh portal to track status or download digital records.

How to fix:

  • Use updated browsers or seek help at Lok Seva Kendras for MP Bhulekh or SAMPADA issues.

Lack of official, on-the-ground demarcation of boundaries leading to disputes with neighbors.

How to fix:

  • Apply for official land demarcation (Seemankan) through the Tehsildar via MP Bhulekh.

Failing to register documents within four months of the sale can lead to severe penalties, sometimes up to 10 times the registration fee.

How to fix:

  • Register the property within the mandatory 4-month window to avoid high penalties.
